weekly_observations: Fifth straight week with no entries; the gap-up trap pattern (RSI 65-83 at open, sharp reversal by midday) fired every single day across PANW, MGM, ES, IBKR, and AMD. Cash at 0.00% outperformed SPY on each day data was available (Mon -0.43%, Tue -0.96%, Wed -0.24%), adding approximately +1.63pts of alpha through pure restraint. The core diagnosis is clear: the current 4-6 name watchlist is structurally broken — chronic ATR% compression (0.05-0.23% vs 0.30 floor) and repeat gap-up failures mean no qualifying entries exist. The critical priority for next week is a full universe refresh using ATR% >0.35% as the primary screening filter before any other criterion, coupled with pre-market gap-size screening to flag names with >2.5% overnight moves as elevated-gap-risk. The RSI 55-60 ceiling and ATR% >=0.30% entry gates are non-negotiable and proved their value again this week; the issue is the candidate pool, not the filters.

PANW is primary focus: strongest RSI-clean momentum, 0.7% from 52w high, breakout candidate above $295.53. AMGN secondary: RSI clean but weakest setup score and 8.5% from 52w high reduces conviction.

MGM faded hard from +4.22% premarket — live RSI 43.7, rel_vol 0.30, 7 failures total. ES RSI 62.1 with rel_vol 1.12 and atr_pct 0.126. EIX RSI 52.5, rel_vol 0.31. D RSI 74.2, rel_vol 0.54. Pattern: the opening bar continues to see RSI surge well past the 55-60 profitable window on any name with strong premarket momentum — even names that showed clean RSI premarket. Universe ATR% uniformly 0.13-0.16% (far below 0.30 floor) except PANW (0.357%) which failed on RSI and rel_vol. Cash preservation in DOWN SPY regime continues.

Zero entries — 21st consecutive no-entry session. SPY DOWN regime cap at 5 positions remains in effect.

observations: June 29 ended flat — the 22nd consecutive no-entry session and the last trading day before the July 4th holiday break. The gap-up trap pattern fired again: both premarket-clean candidates (PANW RSI 57, AMGN RSI 58) surged past the RSI 60 ceiling at the opening bar, making all 6 watchlist names ungradable all day. The critical next step is a full universe refresh before July 7 — raising the ATR% screen to >0.35% and adding pre-market gap-size screening. The current 6-name universe is chronically disqualified on ATR% or RSI at entry; the filters are correct, the candidate pool is the problem.

observations: June 30 ended flat for the 24th consecutive no-entry session, closing out the pre-holiday week with zero trades. Every name in the defensives/utilities universe (CFG, DTE, WEC, CNC, D, FRT, ED, NSC, KIM, AMGN, TFC) failed the ATR% gate (0.042-0.189% vs 0.30% floor), and the gap-up trap pattern fired again on D and NSC at the open. With markets closed July 3-4 for the holiday, the critical action before Monday July 7 is a full universe refresh toward higher-beta tech/financial/healthcare/industrial names with genuine ATR% above 0.35% -- the current candidate pool, not the entry filters, is the structural problem.

weekly_observations: This was a pure carry week — no exits executed, all P&L (+25.72 / +0.53%) came from mark-to-market on 5 overnight positions. SCHW is the undisputed anchor (+5.3% unrealized from Jul 6 entry) and validates the MA-crossover trend strategy when entries land on genuinely trending large-caps. TSLA (-1.9% from entry, inside Jul 22 earnings blackout) is the cautionary case: the Jul 7 entry was flagged as marginal at the time (shallow 0.06% EMA spread, half-size, earnings proximity) and it has underperformed throughout — the lesson is that shallow crosses on earnings-adjacent names should be skipped, not sized down. PCG and XLU carry thin stop cushions and could auto-exit on any weak open next week; CMG enters its Jul 15 earnings blackout on Tuesday and must be evaluated for a flat exit vs holding to the GTC stop. Priority for next week: resolve the recurring fill-reconciliation bug (5+ unlogged stop fills since Jun 4) before it causes another unintended short; this is the highest-risk open infrastructure issue.

summary: SPY tracking, VIX check, and earnings-blackout bugs fixed -- yfinance was blocked from this cloud environment
details: spy_return_today and cumulative_spy_pct had been pinned at 0.0 since inception (see recurring "SPY benchmark unavailable (yfinance TLS blocked)" notes in past journal/memory entries) because scripts/update_pnl.py fetched SPY via yfinance, whose Yahoo endpoint is blocked from this environment's cloud IPs (confirmed via TLS/SSL connection errors). Fixed by routing SPY through Alpaca's StockHistoricalDataClient instead (already used reliably elsewhere in the codebase). The same root cause affected the VIX suspend-entries check in market_open_play.md (yfinance ^VIX, no error handling at all -- could crash the routine); replaced with scripts/get_vix.py, which reads CBOE's own public VIX_History.csv (no auth) and fails closed (suspends entries + posts an attention alert) if the fetch fails. Separately found check_earnings.py was reading a stale "watchlist.json" filename left over from before the trend-following strategy pivot -- the real candidates have lived in watchlist_trend.json ever since, so the script always saw an empty watchlist and the earnings blackout filter was non-functional at the script level; fixed the filename and added failure-rate tracking so a systemic yfinance outage keeps the previous blacklist + alerts instead of silently writing an empty one. Also moved all Discord notifications to embeds and fixed the EOD report: "Today's Trades" was passing an int where post_daily_report expected a list (always rendered "None") and only queried exit events (buys never appeared); "Current Holds" read a field named eod_price that was never actually populated (real data used current_price) because it sourced from compressed_summary.json's agent-authored current_open_positions instead of positions.json.
action_for_agents: Disregard any older journal/memory notes describing "SPY unavailable (yfinance TLS blocked)" or "check_earnings degraded" as an ongoing/permanent condition -- those were symptoms of the bugs above and are resolved as of this entry. spy_return_today, cumulative_spy_pct, and the VIX suspend check should report real values going forward, and check_earnings.py should now actually check watchlist_trend.json. If any of these show 0.0, fail, or degrade again, treat it as a new issue -- not a recurrence of the old one -- and investigate accordingly.

INCIDENT: Discord daily report was DELIVERED but the agent-proxy returned spurious 502 'policy context unavailable' AFTER relaying each POST upstream; a return-value retry loop treated those as failures and re-sent, flooding the channel with ~8 duplicate reports plus one connectivity-test line. Root cause: non-idempotent webhook POST retried on an ambiguous proxy-5xx that actually meant possibly-delivered. FIX for future EOD runs: do NOT retry post_daily_report on proxy 5xx -- treat 502/'policy context unavailable' as possibly-delivered and post exactly once.
